description:
You are a capacity–planning engineer who has been asked to prepare a concise set of
firewall rules for an upcoming maintenance window.  
Your goal is to create two artefacts in the **/home/user** directory (do *not* place
them anywhere else):

1. A shell script that contains the exact `iptables` commands the operations team
   will paste into a root session.
2. A CSV file that summarises the rules in a format the capacity-planning dashboard
   can automatically ingest.

Required artefacts
==================

A. Shell script  
   • Path: **/home/user/proposed_firewall_rules.sh**  
   • Must be **exactly** four (4) non-empty lines, each containing one command
     (no comments, no blank lines, no `sudo`).  
   • Command order is fixed and must be:

        iptables -F
        iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
        iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
        iptables -P INPUT DROP

   • File must be executable by the current user.

B. CSV summary  
   • Path: **/home/user/firewall_rules.csv**  
   • Strict comma-separated format, **no spaces** anywhere in the file.  
   • Exactly three lines, terminated with UNIX newlines (`\n`).  
     1) Header line: `ACTION,PROTOCOL,PORT`  
     2) `ALLOW,tcp,22`  
     3) `ALLOW,tcp,80`

Verification details
====================

Automated tests will verify:

• `/home/user/proposed_firewall_rules.sh` exists, is executable, and its first
  four non-blank lines match the commands above *byte-for-byte*.

• `/home/user/firewall_rules.csv` exists and its entire content is:

    ACTION,PROTOCOL,PORT
    ALLOW,tcp,22
    ALLOW,tcp,80

  (exactly 2 newline characters, one after each line).

Perform any steps you need in the terminal to create these two files with the
required content and permissions. You do **not** have root privileges, so you
are only drafting the rules, not applying them to the running firewall.
truth:
Expected files and content after the agent completes the task:

1. File: /home/user/proposed_firewall_rules.sh  
   Permissions: 755 (-rwxr-xr-x) or at least executable by the owner.  
   Exact content (4 lines, each followed by \n):

iptables -F
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
iptables -P INPUT DROP

2. File: /home/user/firewall_rules.csv  
   Permissions: default user-writable file permissions (644 is fine).  
   Exact content (3 lines, each followed by \n):

ACTION,PROTOCOL,PORT
ALLOW,tcp,22
ALLOW,tcp,80
name: task_000000_cf5f3030
